content.full_text:
TO: CENTRAL FILE
THRU: J. PRITCHARD
FROM: ____________________________  /7 TO BE INCLUDED IN ADVICE PACK
Code §§ and Regulations referenced:

820/3    182/5
84302

Robert Hilton
Attorney-at-Law
Room 6082
600 Grant Street
Pittsburgh, P.A. 15230

Dear Mr. Hilton:

This letter is in response to your telephone inquiry concerning possible filing obligations under the Political Reform Act of California for your client U.S. Steel.

You have stated that U.S. Steel has formed a Political Action Committee which makes contributions to federal candidates and various state candidates throughout the country. You have asked what must be reported under California law when U.S. Steel begins making contributions to California candidates.

In response to this inquiry I am sending you a copy of a letter dated September 15, 1976 to Mr. Thomas I. McKnew, Jr., who is the assistant general attorney for the Atchinsen, Topeka and Santa Fe Railway Company. Mr. McKnew's question to the Commission is very similar to yours and I believe that the response contained in the letter will be an assistance to you.

If you have any additional questions after reading the McKnew letter please do not hesitate to let me know.

Very truly yours,

Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator

RSK:bw
Enclosure
